sentence: Subjective:
The patient, a 62-year-old male, presents for a follow-up visit approximately 4 months after a heart attack and stent placement.
He reports that the medications are helping, but he is concerned about the number of medications he is taking.
He mentions that he is doing okay, but has a complaint of dizziness when standing up quickly.
He also expresses concern about the stents and their lifespan, as well as his diabetes management and kidney function.
He has a history of diabetes and stage 3 chronic kidney disease.
He mentions that his A1C was around 16 and his glucose is around 130 fasting.
He has a preference for San Francisco 49ers football and mentions that he has been lonely since his heart attack.
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 127/80, pulse rate [NOT MENTIONED], oxygen saturation [NOT MENTIONED], temperature [NOT MENTIONED].
Physical exam: The patient has a slight 3/6 systolic ejection murmur heard at the left base and one plus nonpitting edema in the lower extremities.
Test Results:
The patient's kidney function test results show stable kidney function.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has coronary artery disease, stage 3 chronic kidney disease, and diabetes mellitus.
Problem list: Coronary artery disease; stage 3 chronic kidney disease; diabetes mellitus; mild depression; difficulty adjusting after heart attack.
Plan:
I referred the patient for cardiac rehabilitation to increase his activity level and confidence in exercising again.
I will refill his metoprolol 50mg once a day, crestor 40mg once a day, and aspirin.
I will also order another hemoglobin A1c and refer him to a dietitian for dietary education.
I will contact his primary care physician to discuss his insulin regimen.
I will also place a referral to psychiatry for mild depression and difficulty adjusting after heart attack.
Follow-up:
I will contact the patient's nephrologist, Dr. Miller, to schedule an appointment for his stage 3 chronic kidney disease.
I will also check in with the patient to see how he is doing with his cardiac rehabilitation and to address any concerns or questions he may have.
